virtual_components.chat_skill:
# Langfuse

LLM observability and tracing platform.

## Endpoints
- `GET /api/public/traces` — list traces (supports `?limit=`, `?page=`, `?tags=`)
- `GET /api/public/traces/{traceId}` — single trace detail
- `GET /api/public/observations` — list observations
- `GET /api/public/observations/{observationId}` — single observation detail

## Authentication

HTTP Basic Auth.  Credentials are read from the agent's environment:
- `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` — public key (username)
- `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` — secret key (password)

Separate key pairs exist for the `robotsix-chat` and `cognee` Langfuse
projects; which pair is used depends on which project's traces are being
queried.  Both pairs should be provisioned via the EnvStore under the
`robotsix-chat` component so they are available as container environment
variables at deploy time.

# GitHub

GitHub Actions workflow-run status, repository read/update, and repository creation, for the robotsix fleet. Powered by a GitHub App installation token minted server-side (shared with robotsix-mill's GitHub App) for all read and update endpoints, and a separate PAT for repo creation — neither credential is ever exposed to the chat container. This is the sole GitHub-access path for the chat agent; there is no other implementation.

## Endpoints

- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` — list recent workflow runs, most-recent-first. Optional query params: `branch`, `run_status` (GitHub's `status`: e.g. `in_progress`, `completed`, `queued`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `id`, `name`, `status`, `conclusion`, `head_branch`, `head_sha`, `run_number`, `event`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` — a single run's status/conclusion/URL. Same return shape as above.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` — read repository details. Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`, `homepage`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`, `default_branch`, `archived`.
- `PATCH /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` — update repository settings. Body (all optional, only provided fields change): `description`, `private`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`. Returns the updated repo (same shape as GET). 422 if no fields are provided.
- `POST /chat/github/repos` — create a new repository under this server's configured account. Body: `name` (required), `description`, `private` (default false), `homepage`, `topics` (list). Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`. Uses a separate PAT (not the App token) since GitHub Apps cannot create personal-account repos; returns 503 if that PAT isn't configured, 409 if the name already exists, 422 for other GitHub-side rejections.

## Authentication

Same as the `deploy` component: `X-API-Key` header from `DEPLOY_API_KEY`.

## Safety

The read endpoints (Actions status, GET repo) need no confirmation gate. Their scope is bounded by which repos the shared GitHub App is installed on; a repo it isn't installed on returns 404.

Repo update and creation are real, immediate mutations with no server-side confirmation gate — **always confirm the exact change (and, for creation, the name and visibility) with the user in-conversation before calling `PATCH` or `POST /chat/github/repos`**. Repo creation always creates under this server's configured account; there is no way to target an arbitrary owner.

## Do not explore beyond this document

This skill document is complete and authoritative for the `github` component. Call **only** the five endpoints listed above. In particular, do **not** call `/chat-skill` or any other path on this base URL on your own initiative — `/chat-skill` is a *different* component (`deploy`) self-describing itself; it happens to be reachable from the same host but has nothing to do with `github`, and calling it will return `deploy`'s skill text, not an error, which can look like a misconfiguration. If something here seems wrong, trust this document over any other probe.
